info.title: Add build and run commands with test examples to AGENTS.md

diffs.before:
# Agent Guidelines

## Spec-First Workflow

- Read `specs/README.md` before any feature work.
- Assume specs describe intent, not implementation.
- Verify reality in the codebase before claiming something exists.
- Implement to spec patterns and data shapes; update specs only when asked.
- When Writting specs, **NEVER** follow Test Driven Development practices. Write the spec first and stop.

## Testing and Quality Gates

- Follow Test Driven Development practices: write failing tests before implementation.
- Local suite: `bash scripts/quality.sh all`.
- Targeted runs:
  - `bash scripts/quality.sh lint|test|coverage|security|arch`.
- Coverage gate: default min 90% (`COVERAGE_MIN` override).
- Run core tests with `go test ./...`.

## Tooling Expectations

- Go version: 1.25 (see `go.mod`).
- Mutation testing tool: `go-mutesting`.
- Lint and security via `golangci-lint`, `govulncheck`, `go-arch-lint`, `go-fmt`.

## Implementation Guidance

- Keep scans deterministic and reproducible.
- Skip binary/oversized files per spec; record skipped file stats.
- Treat match text as sensitive; avoid logging it in console.
- When multiple code paths do similar work with small variations, consolidate into shared services with request structs.
diffs.after:
# Agent Guidelines

## Spec-First Workflow

- Read `specs/README.md` before any feature work.
- Assume specs describe intent, not implementation.
- Verify reality in the codebase before claiming something exists.
- Implement to spec patterns and data shapes; update specs only when asked.
- When Writting specs, **NEVER** follow Test Driven Development practices. Write the spec first and stop.

## Testing and Quality Gates

- Follow Test Driven Development practices: write failing tests before implementation.
- Local suite: `bash scripts/quality.sh all`.
- Targeted runs:
  - `bash scripts/quality.sh lint|test|coverage|security|arch`.
- Coverage gate: default min 90% (`COVERAGE_MIN` override).
- Run core tests with `go test ./...`.

## Build and Run

- Build the CLI binary: `go build -o bin/reglint ./cmd/reglint`.
- Run from source (no build): `go run ./cmd/reglint <command> [flags]`.
- Example with test data (config + fixture): `go run ./cmd/reglint analyze --config testdata/rules/example.yaml testdata/fixtures`.
- Example with failing config (failOn): `go run ./cmd/reglint analyze --config testdata/rules/fail.yaml testdata/fixtures`.

## Tooling Expectations

- Go version: 1.25 (see `go.mod`).
- Mutation testing tool: `go-mutesting`.
- Lint and security via `golangci-lint`, `govulncheck`, `go-arch-lint`, `go-fmt`.

## Implementation Guidance

- Keep scans deterministic and reproducible.
- Skip binary/oversized files per spec; record skipped file stats.
- Treat match text as sensitive; avoid logging it in console.
- When multiple code paths do similar work with small variations, consolidate into shared services with request structs.

**Checking build and run instructions**

I'm preparing to inspect the README to find build and run commands that can be added to AGENTS.md along with the new commands using apply_patch.

state.output:
<path>/workspaces/regex-checker/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # RegLint
2: 
3: RegLint is a regex-based linter that scans codebases using YAML-defined rules and emits console, JSON, or SARIF outputs for local and CI workflows.
4: 
5: ## Quickstart
6: 
7: ```bash
8: reglint init
9: reglint analyze --config reglint-rules.yaml
10: ```
11: 
12: ## CLI
13: 
14: ```bash
15: reglint <command> [flags]
16: 
17: Commands:
18:   analyze (alias: analyse)
19:   init
20: ```
21: 
22: ## Config
23: 
24: The default rules file name is `reglint-rules.yaml`.
25: 
26: ```bash
27: reglint init --out configs/reglint-rules.yaml
28: reglint analyze --config configs/reglint-rules.yaml
29: ```
30: 
31: ## Outputs
32: 
33: - `console` writes to stdout.
34: - `json` writes to stdout when it is the only format; otherwise set `--out-json`.
35: - `sarif` writes to stdout when it is the only format; otherwise set `--out-sarif`.
36: 
37: ```bash
38: reglint analyze --config reglint-rules.yaml --format console,json --out-json /tmp/scan.json
39: reglint analyze --config reglint-rules.yaml --format sarif --out-sarif /tmp/scan.sarif
40: ```

(End of file - total 40 lines)
</content>
state.title: README.md

metadata.preview:
# RegLint

RegLint is a regex-based linter that scans codebases using YAML-defined rules and emits console, JSON, or SARIF outputs for local and CI workflows.

## Quickstart

```bash
reglint init
reglint analyze --config reglint-rules.yaml
```

## CLI

```bash
reglint <command> [flags]

Commands:
  analyze (alias: analyse)
  init
```

<content>1: // Command reglint provides the CLI entrypoint.
2: package main
3: 
4: import (
5: 	"io"
6: 	"os"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/iyaki/reglint/internal/cli"

10: 
11: var (
12: 	args                   = os.Args
13: 	outputWriter io.Writer = os.Stdout
14: 	exitFunc               = os.Exit
15: )
16: 
17: func main() {
18: 	code := run(args[1:], outputWriter)
19: 	exitFunc(code)
20: }
21: 
22: func run(args []string, out io.Writer) int {
23: 	handlers := map[string]cli.Handler{
24: 		"analyze": cli.HandleAnalyze,
25: 		"init":    cli.HandleInit,
26: 	}
27: 
28: 	return cli.Run(args, handlers, out)
29: }

(End of file - total 29 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: 
3: action="${1:-start}"
4: port="${2:-4096}"
5: 
6: case "$action" in
7: 	start)
8: 		# Start in background so the script returns immediately.
9: 		nohup opencode serve --port "$port" > /tmp/opencode-serve.log 2>&1 &
10: 		;;
11: 	stop)
12: 		if [[ -n "$2" ]]; then
13: 			pkill -f "opencode serve --port $port"
14: 		else
15: 			pkill -f "opencode serve"
16: 		fi
17: 		;;
18: 	*)
19: 		echo "Usage: $0 [start|stop] [port]"
20: 		exit 1
21: 		;;
22: esac

(End of file - total 22 lines)
</content>
state.title: scripts/oc-serve.sh

metadata.preview:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

action="${1:-start}"
port="${2:-4096}"

case "$action" in
	start)
		# Start in background so the script returns immediately.
		nohup opencode serve --port "$port" > /tmp/opencode-serve.log 2>&1 &
		;;
	stop)
		if [[ -n "$2" ]]; then
			pkill -f "opencode serve --port $port"
		else
			pkill -f "opencode serve"
		fi
		;;
	*)
		echo "Usage: $0 [start|stop] [port]"
		exit 1

files.after:
# Agent Guidelines

## Spec-First Workflow

- Read `specs/README.md` before any feature work.
- Assume specs describe intent, not implementation.
- Verify reality in the codebase before claiming something exists.
- Implement to spec patterns and data shapes; update specs only when asked.
- When Writting specs, **NEVER** follow Test Driven Development practices. Write the spec first and stop.

## Testing and Quality Gates

- Follow Test Driven Development practices: write failing tests before implementation.
- Local suite: `bash scripts/quality.sh all`.
- Targeted runs:
  - `bash scripts/quality.sh lint|test|coverage|security|arch`.
- Coverage gate: default min 90% (`COVERAGE_MIN` override).
- Run core tests with `go test ./...`.

## Build and Run

- Build the CLI binary: `go build -o bin/reglint ./cmd/reglint`.
- Run from source (no build): `go run ./cmd/reglint <command> [flags]`.
- Example with test data (config + fixture): `go run ./cmd/reglint analyze --config testdata/rules/example.yaml testdata/fixtures`.
- Example with failing config (failOn): `go run ./cmd/reglint analyze --config testdata/rules/fail.yaml testdata/fixtures`.

## Tooling Expectations

- Go version: 1.25 (see `go.mod`).
- Mutation testing tool: `go-mutesting`.
- Lint and security via `golangci-lint`, `govulncheck`, `go-arch-lint`, `go-fmt`.

## Implementation Guidance

- Keep scans deterministic and reproducible.
- Skip binary/oversized files per spec; record skipped file stats.
- Treat match text as sensitive; avoid logging it in console.
- When multiple code paths do similar work with small variations, consolidate into shared services with request structs.
